question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $112,000 on October 31?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.content: 🟠 Market Overview - Monday, October 20, 2025 Bitcoin opened the week with a steady rise, approaching the first resistance zone at $110,000–112,000. Current price: $110,300–110,400. 📊 Key Metrics 💰 Total Market Cap: $3.69T 🟧 BTC Dominance: 59.62% 🟩 Ethereum: back above $4,000 🔵 Solana: still below $200 but showing strength 🌍 Macro Overview 📉 US Dollar Index (DXY): down to 98.40 📈 Equity Futures: trending higher 🌏 Asian Markets: mostly in the green The $3.55–3.65T market cap zone worked perfectly as support - now the focus shifts to reclaiming $4T and holding above it. evidences.title: VanEck Mid-October 2025 Bitcoin ChainCheck
evidences.content: Global Power Consumption & Mining Difficulty: Bitcoin’s global power consumption (+7%) and mining difficulty (+10%) reached new all-time highs this month, reflecting ongoing mining fleet expansions and ASIC upgrades. Though AI-geared GPUs continue to displace Bitcoin mining ASICs located in key AI data center geographies, miners continue to gravitate towards low-cost power. Transfer Volume from Miners to Exchanges: Transfer volumes from miners to exchanges increased 14% this month versus only 2% in BTC price gains, reflecting miners monetizing their production instead of holding. We think this reflects the steep capex requirements faced by the increasing number of miner AI-pivots as well as de-risking amid price volatility. [...] As of early October 2025, futures leverage sat near its 95th percentile, with the cash collateral backing Bitcoin futures at record highs (~$145B). Open interest peaked at $52B on October 6th before falling to $39B on October 10th following an 8-hour, 20% BTC drawdown, a reminder of how margin calls can cascade through the system. Notably, leverage has never sustained levels above 30% for more than 75 days, suggesting a limit on sustained risk appetite....

evidences.title: Bitcoin Heads into Historically Bullish October After Third- ...
evidences.content: Since 2013, bitcoin has averaged 14.4% gains in October, with a median return of 10.8%. Out of 13 Octobers in that span, 10 ended in the green and only three closed lower. That track record makes October the most statistically favorable month for the asset, with gains often clustering in the second half. Image 2: (CoinGlass) (CoinGlass) Such seasonality is a hallmark of financial markets, with markets tending to recede at the beginning of May before a spike in November. This is based on the belief that equity markets underperform during the summer due to lower trading volumes, reduced institutional activity, and historical returns data. [...] 0.5x 1x 1.25x 1.5x 2x Live 00:05 44:43 44:48 Bitcoin also shows recurring seasonal patterns, often influenced by macro cycles, institutional flows, and retail sentiment. As such, past trading patterns show the first week of October can be choppy or negative, before stronger upside takes hold later in the month. In several years, bitcoin posted double-digit surges after Oct. 15, even when early price action was muted. The backdrop this year is similar. Bitcoin’s mixed performance through 2025 — including drawdowns in spring and uneven summer...

evidences.title: API Client to Binance
evidences.content: binancer 1.2.0 Reference # binancer An R client to the Public Rest API for Binance. API docs: Quick intro to using `binancer` by getting the most recent ~USD price of the cryptocurrencies supported on Binance: ``` library(binancer) library(binancer)binance_coins_prices() binance_coins_prices() ``` Getting data on a specific symbol pair, e.g. the most recent Bitcoin/USDT changes: ``` (klines <- binance_klines('BTCUSDT', interval = '1m'))(klines<- binance_klines('BTCUSDT' = '1m')) ``` Visualize this data, e.g. on a simple line chart: ``` library(ggplot2) library(ggplot2)ggplot(klines, aes(close_time, close)) + geom_line() ggplot(klines aes(close_time close)) + geom_line() ``` Poor man’s candle chart with some `ggplot2` tweaks: [...] library(data.table) library(data.table)klines <- rbindlist(lapply(klines<- rbindlist(lapply( c('ETHBTC', 'ARKBTC', 'NEOBTC', 'IOTABTC'), c('ETHBTC' 'ARKBTC' 'NEOBTC' 'IOTABTC') binance_klines, binance_klines interval = '15m', = '15m' limit = 424)) = 4 24))ggplot(klines, aes(open_time)) + ggplot(klines aes(open_time)) + geom_linerange(aes(ymin = open, ymax = close, color = close < open), size = 2) + geom_linerange(aes(= open = close = close< open) = 2)...

evidences.title: Bitcoin, Ether Struggle With Options Expiry Imminent
evidences.content: Looking ahead, $17 billion worth of bitcoin options expire tomorrow, with a max pain price (the level at which option holders would experience the most financial loss) of $110,000. That is slightly below the current spot price of $112,000 and could provide short-term gravitational pull. For now, bitcoin is expected to consolidate between $110,000 and $116,000 until October. evidences.title: What is Bitcoin Seasonality Chart & How to Use It?
evidences.content: btc seasonality line chart 2020-2025 The BTC seasonality chart above shows Bitcoin’s price performance for every year, starting with 2020. The lines are labeled on the right with the year and returns scored at the end of each. You can see some recurring trends by comparing the overlapping charts, like the rise in BTC prices around October (which did not happen only in 2022). ### Seasonal Returns Calendar [...] ### Seasonal Returns Calendar Another format to present similar recurring trends is a calendar or BTC monthly returns table. It summarizes the Bitcoin historical performance by month in a heatmap-like format. Each cell represents a specific time period (day, week, or month) and is color-coded based on average returns. The most common color code for this is green for positive returns and red for negative returns, sometimes with intensity corresponding to the magnitude of returns. btc seasonal returns calendar This BTC monthly returns chart places years on the rows and months in columns, with a rightmost column summarizing the yearly change. This type of visualization makes it easier to identify which months or periods have historically been strongest or weakest for Bitcoin....

evidences.title: Options on Bitcoin futures - Expiration Date and Strike Price
evidences.content: ## Understanding contract expirations First, let’s look at the contract expirations. CME Group will list six consecutive months of options on Bitcoin futures. Also, there will be two December options contracts. However, if the current year’s December contract is one of the front six consecutive listings – there will be only one additional December listing. Each option will expire into its underlying futures contract, and that futures contract instantaneously settles into cash. For example, assume it’s January. The six consecutive monthly futures listed are: January, February, March, April, May, and June. This year’s December contract and next year’s December contract will also be listed. [...] Additionally, CME will be listing more granular strike intervals as option expiration approaches – again allowing more precision when hedging. Additional strikes may be added based on the movement of the Bitcoin futures price. CME Group will manage the listing daily to ensure that appropriate instruments are available. ## Summary CME Group options on Bitcoin futures provide traders a variety of strikes and expirations. This enables multiple trading strategy “options” to manage bitcoin pric...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's price history (2009-2025) – key market events, data charts ...
evidences.content: While volatility didn't disappear overnight, the tone had shifted. Bitcoin's price jumped noticeably, and the ETF approval sent a strong signal. Institutional access widened significantly, and ties between crypto and the traditional financial system seemed to be deepening. ### 2025: Bitcoin’s current landscape Sixteen years after its launch, Bitcoin has earned a seat at the financial table. As of May 22, 2025, it is trading above $110,00028, marking a significant rise from earlier levels. Trading looks calmer too, with volume and volatility down compared to earlier years, according to Yahoo Finance29 and CoinMarketCap30. [...] ### 2012–2013: Building momentum After the 2011 crash, Bitcoin began climbing again. Prices and trading volume slowly picked up as more people joined the network and exchanges became easier to use. Bitcoin was no longer just for tech-savvy users as it started to reach a broader audience. By 2013, momentum had clearly shifted. Bitcoin passed $100 in April, then hit $200 in October6. In November, it reached $1,000 on Mt. Gox for the first time. Bitcoin started getting real attention. News sites covered it more often, stores and services began accepting it, a...

evidences.content: Analysts discussed bitcoin market positioning, noting that perpetual futures traders had turned more bullish as funding rates shifted from strongly negative to positive. One research view highlighted resistance levels near $75,000 and $85,000 based on on-chain realized price measures, while another analyst said the key shift to monitor was the evolving pricing framework and the effect of elevated energy prices on expectations for monetary easing.

evidences.title: Bitcoin options market positioning and max-pain levels
evidences.content: The strike map suggests heavy call positioning at $100,000 with additional interest at $110,000, $120,000, $130,000, and beyond, while puts are more concentrated in the $70,000-$90,000 range. This implies traders are positioned for upside while also buying downside protection. Near-term max-pain levels cluster in the low-$90,000s, while year-end positioning sits closer to $100,000, reflecting large notional interest at that round number.

evidences.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum options set to expire on Oct. 31
evidences.content: A large batch of Bitcoin and Ethereum options is set to expire on Oct. 31, which could increase market volatility as traders watch positioning, sentiment, and key resistance levels into expiry.
